answer:
The average heat transfer coefficient is defined such that the total heat transfer rate equals \( h_{\text{av}} A (T_s - T)_{\text{av}} \), and also equals the integral of the local heat flux over the surface area. This gives \( h_{\text{av}} = \frac{\int_0^L h_x (T_s - T) \, dx}{L (T_s - T)_{\text{av}}} \), and since \((T_s - T)_{\text{av}} = \frac{(T_s - T_{\text{in}}) + (T_s - T_{\text{out}})}{2}\), the correct expression matches option H.  

\boxed{H}
